title: agent-timeline-cli ingest should reject non-canonical --kind values + suggest the canonical form
description: The wd skill documents canonical kinds per lane: chronicle uses 'winddown.session', journal uses 'winddown.journal', expertise uses 'winddown.expertise'. agent-timeline-cli ingest silently accepts ANY string for --kind, so a typo like 'winddown.chronicle' (which looks plausible but is non-canonical) lands in the ledger without warning. Caught this session: a loop passing --kind "winddown.${lane}" produced winddown.chronicle/winddown.journal/winddown.expertise, the first of which is wrong; had to post-hoc rewrite the last entry's k.t + k.s + k.tg fields via inline python to canonicalize. Resolution: add a kind ordinality table at schema-universal/private/universal/domain/system/schema-families/shinji-techo-schemas/v0.2.0/json/kinds/ enumerating the valid kinds (winddown.session, winddown.journal, winddown.expertise, plus any other shinji-entry-kind values already present in ordinality), and have agent-timeline-cli ingest validate --kind against that table on entry, rejecting unknown kinds with a helpful 'did you mean winddown.session?' suggestion. Pairs with feedback_json-ordinality-tables-not-enums doctrine (closed-condensable catalog → ordinality table, not enum). Extends to Codable validation if/when the kind moves from string to typed enum.
